compiled_state.description:
# S6 — Monitoring, back-out, and live E2E proof + docs

Part of epic 1fa8 (CI as a second Gerrit gate vote via gerrit-to-platform → GitHub Actions).

## Why
The PoC's success criterion is proving the two-vote gate end-to-end on the live Gerrit, with the
operability a real gate needs: failure alarms and a tested back-out. This story activates the gate
(after the voter is proven) and demonstrates the whole flow.

## What
Add a CloudWatch alarm for g2p dispatch + `Verified`-vote failures, a tested back-out, activate the
two-vote submit requirement, run the live end-to-end proof, and document the flow + design notes.

## Scope
- Monitoring (Terraform alarm + `infra/scripts/observability.sh` metric), back-out procedure,
  `CONTRIBUTING.md`, design notes. Gated on S1–S5.
- Out of scope: the individual component builds (S1–S5).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Add a CloudWatch alarm for g2p hook-dispatch failures and `Verified`-vote failures — publish
      the metric from a journald log pattern via `infra/scripts/observability.sh` (modeled on the
      `voter_errors` metric behind `monitoring_s4b.tf`); name the log source, pattern, and metric.
- [ ] Document and TEST a back-out: disabling the `Verified` submit requirement restores
      single-vote (`LLM-Review`-only) gating if g2p/CI is broken.
- [ ] Activate the two-vote submit requirement (authored in S1) now that the voter is proven.
- [ ] Live E2E on the Gerrit host: a red CI blocks submit; `recheck` re-runs; a green change with
      BOTH votes submits and replicates to GitHub `main`. Record the change URL + both votes.
- [ ] Update `CONTRIBUTING.md` (two-vote flow + `recheck`) and capture design notes (cost / latency
      budget, flaky-test handling, `copyCondition` re-run behavior).
compiled_state.title: S6 — Monitoring, back-out, and live E2E proof + docs
